data.title: KelpDAO's rsETH Theft Sparks Concerns Over Asset Security and Market Impact
data.description:
On April 19, crypto influencer benmo.eth highlighted the significant repercussions of the KelpDAO rsETH theft, attributing responsibility to both KelpDAO and LayerZero. According to BlockBeats, the incident may lead to several consequences.

Firstly, the security of wrapped assets like LRT is not comparable to native assets, and lending protocols should not treat them as equivalent collateral. Secondly, LayerZero might lose part of its cross-chain market, as several assets, including usde and usd0, have ceased using its cross-chain solutions. Even if operations resume, restoring credibility will be challenging.

Additionally, Aave's reputation for security has been compromised, bringing the risks of unified lending markets under scrutiny. Each new collateral asset introduces additional risks to existing ones, which is unfair to native assets. Aave V4 and modular lending may become future trends, potentially accelerating related transitions. The market will focus more on the lending business itself rather than individual platforms or curators, although this will increase business costs.

Moreover, the cost for Layer 2 to acquire Total Value Locked (TVL) will rise, with some liquidity potentially returning to Layer 1. Lastly, DeFi may halt its expansion and adopt a more conservative security model while addressing AI-driven security threats like Anthropic Mythos.

data.title: California Judge Dismisses Class-Action Against JENNER Memecoin
data.description: A federal judge in California has dismissed a class-action lawsuit against Caitlyn Jenner's JENNER memecoin. According to NS3.AI, the court ruled that the complaint failed to plausibly demonstrate that the token was an unregistered security under U.S. law. The lawsuit, led by Lee Greenfield, claimed losses exceeding $40,000 on the JENNER token. Judge Stanley Blumenfeld Jr. stated that Jenner's promotion of the token alone did not constitute a common enterprise.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Economist Predicts Long-Term Rise in Gold Prices Despite Recent Decline
data.description: Gold prices have recently faced challenges due to the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict, but the long-term outlook remains positive. According to Jin10, during the '2026 Market Outlook Forum' hosted by the London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG), economist Hong Hao stated that the recent drop in gold prices is not due to deteriorating fundamentals but rather because gold has 'completed a phase of its historical mission.' Hong Hao explained that lower U.S. Treasury credit and higher yields correlate with higher gold prices a year later. Holding 10-year U.S. Treasuries for a year could result in nearly a 10-point loss, making it an unfavorable trade. In contrast, both fundamental logic, narrative logic, and data models point to higher gold prices, with a potential doubling in the future. Despite the short-term significant correction in gold prices, Hong Hao remains optimistic about its long-term prospects.

data.title: LayerZero Addresses rsETH Vulnerability and Collaborates on Resolution
data.description:
On April 19, LayerZero announced its comprehensive understanding of the rsETH vulnerability incident. According to BlockBeats, the company has been actively working with the KelpDAO team to address the issue and continues to monitor the situation. Other applications remain secure.

LayerZero is collaborating with teams such as the Security Alliance to identify the root cause. Once all information is gathered, a complete post-mortem analysis report will be released in conjunction with KelpDAO.

data.title: Aave Implements Emergency Freeze on rsETH Markets Amid Vulnerability Alert
data.description:
On April 19, Aave Guardian initiated an emergency freeze on rsETH and wrsETH markets across all deployments following a warning about a potential vulnerability. According to BlockBeats, all Aave liquidity pools remain secure and operational, with the incident affecting only the rsETH asset itself, not stemming from any flaw in the Aave protocol. The root cause is currently under investigation by KelpDAO, LayerZero, and other teams, with Aave collaborating closely.

In Aave V3, the Guardian has frozen rsETH and wrsETH markets in all deployments. This measure prevents new deposits and borrowing using rsETH as collateral, while existing positions remain unaffected.

In Aave V4, the protocol's security committee has implemented similar protective measures, disabling new supply and borrowing operations related to rsETH. The configuration updates have been executed through Aave Core Hub and Kelp E Spoke.

data.title: Kelp DAO Faces $294 Million Loss in LayerZero Bridge Exploit
data.description: Onchain Lens posted on X that Kelp DAO has suffered a significant loss of approximately $294 million due to a bridge exploit involving LayerZero Core. The incident has led to a sharp decline in the value of $ZRO, which fell from $2 to $1.4. A major investor, referred to as a whale, who held a long position on HyperLiquid, was partially liquidated, incurring a loss of $2,879,854.36. Despite this setback, the whale continues to maintain their position, currently experiencing a floating loss exceeding $750,000. In total, the whale's losses amount to roughly $28.98 million.

data.title: Sonic Labs Investigates $200 Million rsETH Theft
data.description:
Sonic Labs and Flying Tulip founder Andre Cronje announced that his team is actively investigating the L0/rsETH incident. According to Foresight News, preliminary reports suggest that the theft of approximately $200 million in rsETH may have been caused by a private key leak or configuration error. The stolen assets were subsequently deposited into Aave to borrow ETH due to insufficient rsETH liquidity.

Cronje noted that the affected positions are technically still collateralized. However, if bad debt occurs, Aave's token mechanism and safety module will serve as the first line of defense to absorb the risk. Aave does not have a mechanism to subsidize user losses, as this could trigger a bank run. Currently, Aave holds around $7 billion in ETH and has a withdrawal capacity of about $100 million, indicating that the overall impact of this incident is limited.

To prioritize user liquidity, Flying Tulip has withdrawn all ETH from Aave into its fund management encapsulation contract. This decision was made because Aave's available liquidity has fallen below its minimum threshold.

data.title: KelpDAO Exploiter Among Top Holders on Aave and Arbitrum
data.description: PeckShieldAlert posted on X that the KelpDAO exploiter has become a significant holder on both Aave and Arbitrum platforms. The exploiter is currently the eighth largest holder of variableDebtEthWETH on Aave, holding 52,443.94 units, valued at approximately $123 million. Additionally, they are the fourth largest holder of variableDebtarbWETH on Arbitrum, with 12,381.93 units, valued at around $22 million. In total, the exploiter's holdings amount to 106,466.7 ETH, which is approximately $250 million.

data.title: TRUMP Tokens Transferred to Centralized Exchange
data.description: TRUMP tokens have been moved to a centralized exchange, according to Foresight News. Onchain Lens monitoring revealed that the TRUMP token team transferred 7.59 million TRUMP tokens to a BitGo custody address. These tokens, valued at approximately $21.81 million, were subsequently moved to a centralized exchange.

data.title: Curve Founder Highlights Risks in Non-Isolated Lending Models
data.description:
Curve founder Michael Egorov has expressed concerns over the recent attack on Kelp DAO, which resulted in losses of approximately $292 million and led to bad debt formation on Aave. According to Foresight News, Egorov attributes these issues to the prevalent use of non-isolated lending models, which, while offering good scalability, come with higher risks. He emphasizes the importance of risk management in such models.

Egorov suggests that adopting a fully isolated model, similar to Curve Finance's market, or a hybrid model, though complex, could mitigate these risks. However, he notes that the market has yet to fully understand the advantages of these approaches. The Hub and Spoke model of Aave v4 is seen as a potential step towards a semi-isolated and safer direction.

data.title: Lido: EarnETH deposits have been suspended due to the Kelp security incident; stETH and the staking protocol are unaffected.
data.description: Lido posted on the X platform that he is aware of the Kelp DAO security incident. Due to the current exposure of the earnETH product to rsETH, new deposits to earnETH have been suspended as a precaution. He is currently assessing the situation with relevant partners and will disclose more information as it develops. Lido added that stETH and wstETH were not affected by the rsETH incident, and the security issue does not involve the Lido staking protocol itself.

data.title: Curve founder: KelpDAO attack causes Aave bad debts, exposing risks of non-segregated lending.
data.description: Curve founder Michael Egorov stated that the attack on KelpDAO resulted in approximately $292 million in losses and bad debts on Aave, reflecting the potential risks of the current "non-segregated lending" model. He pointed out that while this model has good scalability, it carries high risk and requires strengthened risk management. He also mentioned that fully segregated or hybrid models may be viable alternatives, and believes that the "hub and spoke" architecture proposed by Aave v4 may drive lending models towards a more secure direction.

data.title: ASTEROID's market capitalization surpasses $150 million, with a daily increase of over 550%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, ASTEROID's market capitalization briefly surged past $150 million, currently trading at $146 million, representing a daily increase of over 550%. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: KelpDAO Suffers Major Exploit Resulting in Significant Losses
data.description:
KelpDAO experienced a significant security breach on April 19, resulting in the unauthorized creation of 116,500 rsETH tokens. According to BlockBeats, the attack led to losses estimated at approximately $293 million.

The majority of the illicitly minted rsETH was deposited into Aave as collateral to borrow ETH, while a smaller portion was directly sold for ETH. The hacker managed to acquire a total of 106,466 ETH through these actions.

data.title: Aave Freezes rsETH Markets Following KelpDAO Bridge Exploit
data.description: Aave has temporarily frozen its rsETH markets in response to a significant exploit involving the KelpDAO bridge, which resulted in an estimated $177 million to $200 million in bad debt within Aave's wETH pool. According to NS3.AI, Omer Goldberg from Chaos Labs reported that the attacker managed to borrow 83,427 WETH and wstETH using unbacked rsETH across both Ethereum and Arbitrum networks. Following the news of the exploit, AAVE's price experienced an intraday decline of approximately 10%. Despite the incident, Aave confirmed that its contracts were not compromised and that withdrawals remain operational.

data.title: Lido Suspends EarnETH Deposits Following Kelp DAO Attack
data.description: Lido has announced the suspension of further deposits into earnETH due to exposure to rsETH following the Kelp DAO attack. According to Foresight News, the Lido Earn team is actively assessing the situation with relevant partners. Lido has assured users that stETH and wstETH remain unaffected by the rsETH incident, and the staking protocol continues to operate normally.

data.title: Ethereum's Kelp Protocol Suspends rsETH Contracts Amid Investigation
data.description: Ethereum's restaking protocol, Kelp, has confirmed suspicious cross-chain activity involving rsETH. According to Odaily, the protocol has paused rsETH contracts on the mainnet and several Layer 2 networks. An investigation into the incident has been initiated, with Kelp collaborating with LayerZero, Unichain, auditing firms, and security experts to conduct a root cause analysis. Further details will be released following the investigation.

data.title: Kelp Suspends rsETH Contracts Amid Suspicious Cross-Chain Activity
data.description: Kelp has temporarily halted rsETH contracts on the Ethereum mainnet and several Layer 2 networks following the detection of suspicious cross-chain activity. According to NS3.AI, the company has initiated an investigation into the potential attack and is collaborating with LayerZero, Unichain, audit firms, and security experts to conduct a thorough root cause analysis. Kelp plans to publish a post-mortem report once the investigation is complete.

data.title: Andre Cronje: Aave has no subsidy-based user loss mechanism and has withdrawn ETH to the fund management encapsulation contract.
data.description: Andre Cronje, co-founder of Sonic Labs and founder of Flying Tulip, stated on the X platform that his team is continuously investigating the L0/rsETH incident. Preliminary reports indicate that approximately $200 million worth of rsETH was stolen, possibly due to a private key leak or misconfiguration. These assets were subsequently deposited into Aave as collateral for ETH lending (due to insufficient rsETH liquidity). Cronje pointed out that the affected positions are technically still collateralized, but in the event of bad debts, Aave's token mechanism and security modules will act as the first line of defense to absorb the risk. However, Aave does not have a mechanism to compensate users for losses; otherwise, it could trigger a run on the platform. Currently, Aave holds approximately $7 billion worth of ETH and has about $100 million in withdrawals, so the overall impact of this incident is limited. Furthermore, prioritizing user liquidity, Flying Tulip has withdrawn all ETH from Aave to its fund management contract. This action was taken because Aave's available liquidity had fallen below its set minimum threshold.

data.title: Aave Chan Initiative Halts Frontier Staking Service Amid Risks
data.description:
Aave Chan Initiative (ACI) founder Marc Zeller announced on the governance forum the immediate termination of the Frontier staking service plan due to concerns over rsETH collateral and potential wETH shortages and bad debt risks in Aave V3 Core. According to Foresight News, all ETH staked through Frontier will be fully withdrawn and managed by Aave DAO to protect wETH depositors if necessary. Additionally, ACI has decided to forgo all potential income and planned fees from the service until the exit is completed.

Frontier was introduced by ACI in January 2024 as a staking-as-a-service product for Aave DAO.
